description_long: This is a four-story apartment building with a pale yellow facade and a sloped, light gray metal roof. The front side features regularly spaced windows and small balconies with dark railings, giving the building a symmetrical and orderly appearance. The rear side has two main entrances with concrete steps and a series of evenly distributed windows, but no balconies. The side elevations are plain, with only windows and no additional architectural features. The roof includes several chimneys and vent pipes, indicating a functional residential design. The overall structure is rectangular, with a utilitarian yet slightly classical urban residential style.
description: A four-story yellow apartment building with a sloped gray roof, balconies on the front, and multiple windows arranged symmetrically.
description_view_0: The front view shows the building's yellow facade with six small balconies and three rows of windows, all symmetrically arranged.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain yellow wall with only windows and no balconies or entrances.
description_view_2: The rear view displays two main entrances with steps and a series of windows, but no balconies.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a plain wall with windows and no additional features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a highly symmetrical grid pattern, with equal spacing both vertically and horizontally across all facades.
